Details: One study showed that chlortetracycline strongly adsorbed to soils, with a preference for clays, followed by humic substances and clay-humic complexes [1523]. Humic substances in clay-humic complexes appear to reduce sorption by masking sorption sites or by inhibiting interlayer diffusion [1523]. Sorption was greater to calcium-saturated soil than potassium-saturated soil [1523]. Sorption decreased with increasing pH, suggesting that cation bridging and cation exchange contribute to sorption [1523].
